rolloutHazards.reason: Must ship atomic — splitting creates a transient-inconsistency window where description.json.lastUpdated is fresh but graph-metadata.derived.last_save_at still lags under default plan-only saves
Atomic ship or feature-flag — otherwise trustState vocabulary splits across code-graph family and naive consumers interpret missing-trustState siblings as failed
MUST precede T-W1-HOK-01 to avoid Copilot re-inlining wrapRecoveredCompactPayload as a 3rd duplicate that HOK-02 then must remove
Must land together — SAN-03 NFKC tests fail red until SAN-01 adds normalizePrompt NFKC code
16-folder write sweep — never run until every preceding writer (CNS-01, W1-CNS-04, CNS-02, W1-CNS-05) is verified live via a 1-folder smoke test
Must land AFTER T-SCP-01 in same PR — lint-first-before-collapse breaks the build on existing 4 duplicates
Must not activate in --strict mode before 170/179 malformed markers in the 016 checklist.md are rewrapped — otherwise the validating packet fails its own lint

resolution: Merge into one PR — same 75-line span, two related fixes
CGC-02 merged into CGC-01 or dropped — per review §5 CGC-02 is partial-mitigation-if-CGC-01-deferred
Serialize: PIN-RET-01 (surgical) → T-RBD-03 (comment-only) → T-PIN-GOD-01 (large extraction) → T-W1-PIN-02 (satisfies in already-extracted helper)
Serialize: HOK-02 extracts wrapRecoveredCompactPayload first, then SAN-02 adds NFKC to the smaller sanitizeRecoveredPayload that remains
Batch 3 lint-rule additions into one validate.sh commit to avoid 3 serial merges on same file

coupling: Code-before-test — SAN-03 NFKC/unicode cases fail red until SAN-01 adds normalizePrompt NFKC
New test asserts lastUpdated monotonic advancement — cannot land before CNS-01 + W1-CNS-04
New test added before PIN-GOD-01 refactor to harden rollup-divergence intent before the big extraction

resolution: Dependency graph has one dominant spine (Cluster B infrastructure: workflow.ts writers → research-backfill → code-graph readiness-contract → continuity-validator → 16-folder sweep, 7 nodes, ~50h) and three independent parallel lanes (Cluster C sanitization; Cluster A + SRS auth + PIN-RET-01; hooks-runtime parity HOK-02→HOK-01). 4-wave plan serialises spine in Wave A→B→C, runs lanes parallel to Wave B, defers Wave D P2s without blocking Phase 017 ship. 7 rollout hazards identified, 5 file-collision groups, 3 test-coupling chains.

direction: Land T-CNS-03 first to expose pipeline gaps
reason: CNS-03 is a write-sweep; running before writers land produces stale data that persists until next sweep

direction: Parallelize T-CGC-01 and T-W1-CGC-03 via concurrent writes to lib/code-graph/readiness-contract.ts
reason: Shared module API is defined by CGC-01's refactor of context.ts; W1-CGC-03 consumes that API; parallel drift risks API split

direction: Defer T-W1-HOK-02 shared-provenance extraction to post-Phase-017
reason: If HOK-01 lands before HOK-02, Copilot re-inlines wrapRecoveredCompactPayload as 3rd duplicate; cheaper to extract first

direction: Ship T-EXH-01 (assertNever + 8 unions) in Wave A
reason: L-effort, 8+ files, zero infrastructure dependency; belongs in parallel Wave D

candidate1: KQ-51-6 — verify W1-CNS-04 full-auto default flip does not wipe custom manual.* fields across 16 sibling folders
candidate2: KQ-51-7 — test-coverage debt for 8 Wave-1-derived tasks (do existing tests cover them or is there debt for Wave B)
candidate3: KQ-51-8 — convergence check: is segment-2 newFindingsRatio below threshold at iter 54?
recommendation: KQ-51-8 is most defensible — if converged, pivot to segment-2 synthesis iteration; otherwise dispatch KQ-51-6 or KQ-51-7
